title: 11 Herbal Tinctures to Give Asthmatics Relief from Itchy Eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus tinctures are a natural remedy that can help alleviate itchy eyes. This is due to the presence of a compound called ingenol mebutate, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When applied topically or taken internally in the form of a tincture, it can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The tincture's active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work together to calm the irritated tissues and promote healing. If you're considering using Euphorbia peplus tinctures for itchy eyes, start by consulting with a healthcare professional, especially if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking medications. To use the tincture, you can add a few drops to water or another liquid and drink it, or apply a few drops directly to the affected area. However, be sure to follow the recommended dosage and instructions carefully, as excessive consumption can cause stomach upset. When taken as directed, Euphorbia peplus tinctures may help to reduce the severity and frequency of itchy eyes. You may start to notice an improvement in symptoms within a few hours, with the inflammation and redness subsiding as the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties take effect. In some cases, the tincture may also help to prevent the spread of infections that can cause itchy eyes. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, you should seek medical attention to rule out any underlying conditions that may require further treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Euphorbia peplus herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures are a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy eyes. This is because the tincture contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, thanks to its high concentration of flavonoids, terpenoids, and carotenoids. These compounds work together to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, making them a popular choice for people who experience itchy eyes due to allergies, dryness, or minor eye infections.  To use Calendula officinalis tinctures for itchy eyes, start by washing your hands thoroughly before handling the tincture. Then, soak a clean cloth in the tincture and gently apply it to your closed eyes for a few minutes. You can also add a few drops of the tincture to a warm compress and apply it to your eyes.  When you apply the tincture, you may experience a slight stinging sensation as the antiseptic properties work to kill off any underlying infections. However, this should subside quickly, and you should start to feel a cooling and soothing effect as the inflammation and irritation are reduced. You may also notice that your eyes feel more comfortable and less itchy over time. It's essential to use the tincture as directed and to follow up with proper eye care, such as washing your eyes with cool water and using artificial tears if necessary.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures have been traditionally used to alleviate itchy eyes, and it's not hard to see why. The plant contains compounds like allantoin and mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Allantoin helps to reduce swelling and itching, while mucilages form a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, providing relief from irritation. When you use Symphytum officinale tinctures for itchy eyes, start by adding a few drops to a clean cloth or cotton pad and gently apply it to the affected area. You can also take the tincture sublingually (under your tongue) as directed, usually 2-3 times a day. As you use the tincture, you may notice a decrease in the intensity and frequency of the itching, and your eyes may begin to feel more comfortable and calm. The mucilages in the tincture can also help to reduce redness and puffiness, leaving your eyes looking clearer and more refreshed.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures have been used to help alleviate itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, particularly from the presence of alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These constituents work together to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes. When using Echinacea purpurea tinctures for itchy eyes, start by adding a few drops to a glass of water and then gently rinse your eyes with the solution.  You can also apply a few drops directly to the closed eyelids, but make sure to wash your hands thoroughly beforehand to avoid transferring any bacteria. Some people find that using a cotton swab to apply the tincture helps to target the affected area more precisely. It's essential to dilute the tincture with water before applying it to the eyes, as the concentrated form can be too intense.  When you apply the diluted tincture to your itchy eyes, you may notice a reduction in the burning sensation and itching. Your eyes may also feel more comfortable and less swollen. However, it's crucial to note that Echinacea purpurea tinctures may not completely eliminate the itchy eyes, but they can provide temporary relief.  If your itchy eyes persist or worsen, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. They may recommend other remedies or further testing to determine the underlying cause of your itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures are a natural remedy for itchy eyes, and they work by soothing and calming the eyes. The tincture is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which is rich in compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes. When you apply the tincture to your itchy eyes, the soothing properties of aloe vera help to calm the skin and reduce the itching.  To use aloe barbadensis tincture for itchy eyes, you can apply a few drops to a cotton pad or a clean cloth and gently place it over your closed eyes. You can also dilute the tincture with water and use it as an eye wash. You can repeat this process as often as needed, but be careful not to overuse it, as this can cause dryness and irritation. When you apply the tincture, you may notice a reduction in the redness and swelling of your eyes, as well as a decrease in the itching. The soothing properties of aloe vera can also help to calm the skin and reduce the risk of infection. However, if your itchy eyes persist or are accompanied by other symptoms like discharge or vision problems, you should consult a doctor to rule out any underlying conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major tinctures have been used to help with itchy eyes for centuries, and it's not hard to see why. The plant contains properties like aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory effects, and aucubin, a compound that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. These constituents work together to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing the itchiness and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.  If you're experiencing itchy eyes and want to try Plantago major tinctures, start by placing a few drops of the tincture into your eyes 2-3 times a day. You can also mix the tincture with water and use it as an eye wash. Be sure to dilute the tincture properly, as undiluted tinctures can be quite strong. Taking Plantago major tinctures for itchy eyes may lead to a reduction in inflammation and swelling, making it easier to tolerate the discomfort. You may also notice an improvement in the overall health of your eyes, with less redness and tearing. However, if your itchy eyes persist or worsen, you should consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago major herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures can be a helpful remedy for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Aloe vera contains compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E that work together to calm and protect the eyes. These constituents help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, providing quick relief from itchy eyes. If you're experiencing itchy eyes, try adding 10-20 drops of aloe vera tincture to a glass of water and drinking it several times a day. Alternatively, you can apply a few drops directly to the affected eye, but be sure to wash your hands first. You can also add aloe vera tincture to a warm compress to help soothe and calm the eyes. After using aloe vera tincture, you can expect to experience a reduction in eye irritation and itching. The swelling and redness should decrease, allowing your eyes to feel more comfortable. In severe cases, you may notice a significant decrease in eye discharge or tearing. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita tinctures are a herbal remedy that can help soothe itchy eyes. The main active compounds in Chamomilla recutita, also known as German chamomile, are apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes. When you apply Chamomilla recutita tinctures to your eyes, the apigenin and luteolin bind to the histamine receptors in the eyes, reducing the allergic response that causes itching. If you're experiencing itchy eyes, you can try the following: Soak a cotton ball in the tincture and gently apply it to your closed eyes for a few minutes. You can also add a few drops of the tincture to a cup of warm water and use it as an eye bath. Be sure to dilute the tincture with water, as it can be quite potent. It's also essential to do a patch test before using the tincture to ensure you're not allergic to it. If you experience any discomfort or irritation, discontinue use. When you use Chamomilla recutita tinctures for itchy eyes, you can expect to experience relief from itching and inflammation. The tincture may also help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. However, the effects may not be immediate, and it may take some time to notice a difference. Additionally, the tincture may interact with other medications, so be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tinctures are a natural remedy for itchy eyes, and it works by targeting the underlying causes of the discomfort. This tincture contains properties like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These constituents help to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.  To use Euphrasia officinalis tinctures effectively, start by washing your hands thoroughly before handling the product. Then, put a few drops of the tincture into a clean water bottle or glass of water. You can also add a few drops to a warm compress and apply it to your closed eyes for a few minutes.  After taking action, you may experience a reduction in eye irritation and itchiness. The tincture may also help to soothe and calm the eyes, making them feel more comfortable. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for further guidance.
remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures, also known as yarrow, contain compounds like chamazulene and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties can help with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and blocking the release of histamine, a chemical that causes allergic reactions.  If you're experiencing itchy eyes, try taking 10-20 drops of Achillea millefolium tincture in a glass of water, 2-3 times a day. You can also add a few drops to a warm compress and apply it to your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes.  Taking Achillea millefolium tinctures may help reduce the itching and redness in your eyes, and you may start to feel relief within a few hours. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, you should consult with a healthcare professional for further guidance.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata tinctures may help with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties, specifically aucubin and plantagin, which are known to reduce swelling and calm irritation. This plant also has antihistamine and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and prevent infections that can cause itchy eyes. When using Plantago lanceolata tinctures for itchy eyes, take a few drops of the tincture and mix it with water or another lubricating eye drop to dilute it, then gently apply it to the affected eye.  You can also try soaking a clean cloth in the tincture and applying it over your closed eyes for a few minutes. Repeat this process a few times a day until your symptoms improve. Keep in mind that you should not use the tincture directly in your eyes, as it can cause irritation. If your symptoms persist or worsen, you may want to consult with a healthcare professional for further guidance.  As you start using the tincture, you might experience a decrease in itchy sensations and redness in your eyes within a few hours. You may also notice an improvement in your eye discharge or a reduction in puffiness around your eyes. However, if you have any underlying eye conditions or are taking medications, you should consult with a healthcare professional before using Plantago lanceolata tinctures.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago lanceolata herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

intro_desc: For people with asthma, itchy eyes can be a real problem. When you have asthma, your airways are always on high alert, ready to react to any trigger that might set off a flare-up. This can cause you to rub your eyes a lot, which can make the itchiness worse. But it's not just the itchiness that's the issue - it's also the fact that you might be using asthma medication that can dry out your eyes, making them even more itchy. This can create a vicious cycle that's hard to break. When your eyes are itchy, it's hard to focus on anything else, and it can be especially frustrating if you're trying to work, study, or just enjoy your daily activities. It's like having a constant itch in the back of your mind, always there but never quite scratchable.  As you can imagine, itchy eyes can be really debilitating for people with asthma. It's not just about the physical discomfort - it's also about the emotional toll it takes. When you're constantly dealing with itchiness, you can start to feel anxious, stressed, and irritable. It's like having a weight on your shoulders, always feeling like you're on edge. And if you're taking medication to control your asthma, you might be worried about the potential side effects on your eyes.  Now, some people are turning to herbal tinctures as a way to treat itchy eyes. These tinctures are made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm down itchy eyes. They work by reducing inflammation and calming down the nerves that are causing the itchiness. Some people find that these tinctures give them relief from itchy eyes, and that's a big deal for people with asthma. By using a natural remedy, they can avoid the side effects of medication and get back to their normal activities.
